frameworks.name: uncertainty-directed exploration
frameworks.one_line: choice driven by reducing uncertainty over option values

frameworks.name: feature-based generalization
frameworks.one_line: learned value generalizes across options via shared features

frameworks.name: computational models of exploration
frameworks.one_line: lab models tested at real-world consumer-choice scale

core_question: Do computational models of exploration generalize from lab tasks to real-world consumer choice?
key_assumptions: exploration trades off uncertainty reduction vs immediate value
value can be generalized across options sharing features
order history reflects deliberate choice under uncertainty
manipulations: naturalistic variation in customers, restaurants, and orders
comparison of model variants (uncertainty-directed vs alternatives)
key_findings: customers show hallmarks of adaptive exploration in real orders
feature-based generalization guides which options are explored
interpretations: lab-derived exploration models scale to complex real-world choice
people use sophisticated structured strategies, not random exploration
